betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Particle Chamber: Target Chamber§f

§aTarget Chambers convert particle beams into new particles, items, and fluids!§f
The §bTarget Chamber§f is a square-hollow multiblock that fires an incoming particle beam at a central target block, triggering §bTarget Chamber Recipes§f defined in `lach_01298.qmd.recipes.TargetChamberRecipes.java`. Each recipe requires a §bSilicon Wafer§f, ingot/dust, or other item, plus an incoming particle beam of the right species, energy, and focus.

§6Multiblock Structure (TargetChamberLogic.java:64)§f:
- §bExterior§f: any size NxNxM (LengthX = LengthZ, square footprint), but §cLengthX must be odd§f (3, 5, 7, …).
- §bCenter block§f: a §bParticle Chamber§f (target) tile at the geometric middle.
- §bBeam ports§f: one or more §bParticle Chamber Beam Ports§f on the four outer faces, each connected inward to the center by a line of §bParticle Chamber Beam Blocks§f.
- §bExactly one§f beam port must be set to §bINPUT§f (others may be §bOUTPUT§f or §bDISABLED§f).
- §bAt least 1§f §bParticle Chamber Energy Port§f (supplies RF).
- §bAt least 1§f §bParticle Chamber Fluid Port§f for fluid input/output.
- §cHollow interior§f: only the target block, beam blocks, beam ports, energy ports, and fluid ports are allowed inside; the multiblock's PART_BLACKLIST forbids other chamber parts.

§6Controller Recipe§f:
The §bTarget Chamber Controller§f (`qmd:target_chamber_controller`) is crafted in a §bminecraft.crafting§f table from:
- §bElite Plating§f (`nuclearcraft:part:3`)
- §bTough Alloy Ingot§f (`nuclearcraft:alloy:1`)
- §bBasic Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:4`)
- §bParticle Chamber Casing§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_casing`)

§6Recipe Example — P-Type Doped Silicon§f:
Per `TargetChamberRecipes.java:830`:
- Input 1: §bSilicon Wafer§f (`qmd:semiconductor:3`, 1)
- Input 2: §bBoron-11 ion§f (1 particle, mean energy §e≥ 600 keV§f, focus §e≥ 2.0§f)
- Output: §bP-Type Doped Silicon§f (`qmd:semiconductor:0`)
- RF cost: 1,000 per wafer; efficiency 1.0; heat 0.

§6Common Recipe Categories§f:
- §bProton activation§f: Silicon Wafers, Boron, Lithium, Beryllium → doped silicon, alpha/neutron/proton byproducts
- §bNeutron activation§f: Graphite, Hafnium, Bismuth, Lead → activated isotopes + secondary particles
- §bAntiproton / antineutron production§f: high-energy proton beams on heavy targets → antimatter

§cWarning§f: A Target Chamber that is missing a target block, has no INPUT beam port, has no energy port, or is not square/odd-sized will fail the `isMachineWhole()` validation and refuse to form.

§eTip§f: Use the §bBeam Meter§f tool to inspect the incoming beam at the Target Chamber's input port before you turn the chamber on — make sure the particle species, energy, and focus match the recipe's requirements, or the chamber will consume RF and produce heat without making progress.
